food_name: Nasi Liwet Solo (Solo Coconut Rice with Chicken and Egg)
aliases: nasi liwet with chicken opor and egg

english: nasi liwet with chicken opor and egg

food_subgroup: Coconut rice with chicken opor, egg in coconut sauce, and papaya vegetable

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Nasi Liwet Solo. Closest parent is coconut milk rice with multiple Javanese accompaniments (chicken opor, areh egg, stir-fried young papaya). No exact FoodOn URI can be assigned without fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coconut rice with Javanese chicken curry, egg in coconut sauce, and accompaniments (Solo nasi liwet style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00PB (rice-based mixed dish) is the closest defensible parent. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Nasi Liwet Solo as a named dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Nasi Liwet Solo or its closest parent as a named Indonesian dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Nasi Liwet Solo weighs approximately 400g, comprising coconut rice, chicken opor pieces, one areh-bathed hard-boiled egg, and stir-fried young papaya or chayote, served on a banana leaf.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Liwet Solo — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table; values estimated from component ingredients (coconut rice, chicken opor, hard-boiled egg, young papaya stir-fry)
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for Nasi Liwet Solo as a composite dish. Values are estimated from component-level data in the FAO/INFOODS SEA table: coconut milk rice (~55% of dish weight), chicken in coconut milk opor (~25%), hard-boiled egg in areh (~12%), and stir-fried young papaya/chayote (~8%). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-check for individual ingredient macros (rice, egg, chicken, coconut milk).

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — coconut milk rice analogous estimate; GI ~68 based on jasmine rice cooked with coconut milk (GI modestly reduced vs plain white rice GI ~72–73 by fat and protein from coconut milk)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nasi Liwet Solo. GI of 68 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for coconut milk rice (closest analogous food: nasi lemak / coconut rice base, GI ~68). The large rice portion (approximately 220g cooked rice per 400g plate) dominates the glycemic response. GL per 400g serving = (68 × 86g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 58.5. This is one of the highest GL values per plate among Indonesian rice dishes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 285mg per 100g; a standard 400g plate delivers approximately 1,140mg sodium (47% of the 2,400mg daily limit)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume Nasi Liwet Solo occasionally but should request a smaller rice portion and avoid adding extra salt or sambal, as a full 400g plate already provides nearly half the recommended daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ~58.5 per 400g plate) driven by a large coconut rice portion (~220g cooked rice); medium GI of 68 is only modestly reduced from plain white rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Nasi Liwet Solo to a half-portion (approximately 200g) to keep the glycemic load below 30, pair it with extra stir-fried papaya or chayote for fibre, and monitor postprandial blood glucose closely given the high carbohydrate load from the coconut rice base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (6.2g per 100g; approximately 24.8g per 400g plate) from coconut milk in both the rice and the chicken opor component, plus cholesterol 62mg per 100g (248mg per plate)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Nasi Liwet Solo to occasional consumption (no more than once per week), request a smaller coconut rice portion, and choose extra papaya or chayote vegetable to increase fibre and antioxidant intake, as the high saturated fat from coconut milk significantly elevates LDL cholesterol risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (6.2g/100g) from coconut milk may promote pro-inflammatory pathways; papaya/chayote component provides anti-inflammatory vitamin C (5.5mg/100g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Nasi Liwet Solo to once per week, request a smaller coconut rice portion, and choose extra papaya or chayote vegetable for its anti-inflammatory vitamin C and antioxidant content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Liwet Solo is a distinctly Central Javanese dish originating from the royal city of Solo (Surakarta), Indonesia. Unlike Jakarta's Nasi Uduk — which uses lemongrass and pandan in coconut rice — Nasi Liwet Solo's rice is simmered in a combination of coconut milk, chicken broth, salam leaf (Indonesian bay leaf), and lemongrass until the grains are fragrant, creamy, and slightly sticky. The dish is traditionally served on a banana leaf and comprises three main accompaniments: (1) chicken pieces braised in a mild white coconut milk gravy called opor, (2) a hard-boiled egg bathed in thick areh — a reduced, sweetened coconut cream sauce unique to Javanese cuisine — and (3) stir-fried young papaya or chayote vegetable seasoned with garlic and shallots. Nutritionally, Nasi Liwet Solo is energy-dense at approximately 188 kcal per 100g, with a macronutrient profile of 7.5g protein, 9.5g fat (of which 6.2g is saturated fat from coconut milk), and 21.5g carbohydrate. A standard 400g plate delivers approximately 752 kcal, 86g carbohydrate, and 24.8g saturated fat. The glycemic index is estimated at 68 (medium) with a glycemic load of approximately 58.5 per plate — among the highest per-plate GL values in Indonesian cuisine. Clinically, the dish poses high concern for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease due to its large rice portion and high coconut milk saturated fat content. Sodium is moderate at 285mg per 100g. Culturally, Nasi Liwet Solo is considered an aristocratic royal cuisine of Central Java, traditionally sold from dawn by female vendors (bakul) carrying it in clay pots (kendil). It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: nasi liwet Solo vs nasi lemak

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Nasi Liwet Solo as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA component-level data for coconut rice, chicken opor, hard-boiled egg, and stir-fried young papaya, cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using coconut milk rice as the closest match. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Nasi Liwet Solo as a composite dish is not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA table. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for traditional coconut-milk-based preparations with no hydrogenated fats; consistent with FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy data for coconut milk and chicken. All core macros (protein_g 7.5, fat_g 9.5, carbohydrate_g 21.5) are non-zero and reflect the composite dish profile. Glycemic load per 400g serving calculated as (GI 68 × 86g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 58.5. The input description field was truncated in the source record; the full description has been reconstructed from the llm_context natural_language_description field in the input.
